title: Cloudflare Pages Deployment
deliverable: niyet.burakgizlice.com is live, auto-deploys on push to main, PR preview deployments work, service worker is served correctly, and Supabase Auth redirect URLs include both the production domain and *.pages.dev previews.

in: Connect GitHub repo to Cloudflare Pages with framework preset Vite, build command `npm run build`, output directory `dist`
SPA fallback: `public/_redirects` file with `/* /index.html 200` so client-side routing and hard refresh resolve to index.html
Environment variables VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY configured in Cloudflare Pages → Settings → Environment Variables (both Production and Preview environments)
Custom domain niyet.burakgizlice.com added in Cloudflare Pages → Custom Domains, DNS CNAME record pointing to the Pages project
Auto-deploy triggered on every push to the `main` branch
Preview deployments auto-created for every pull request (branch deployments)
Supabase Auth → URL Configuration → Redirect URLs updated to include https://niyet.burakgizlice.com/** and https://*.pages.dev/**
Verify service worker from Block 15 is served with correct Content-Type and caching headers from Cloudflare Pages
Verify PWA manifest is accessible and installable on the production domain
Deploy checklist document (in this spec) covering every manual configuration step
Smoke-test suite covering load, routing, SW registration, and auth redirect
out: Paid Cloudflare plans or Workers — free Cloudflare Pages tier only
CI/CD beyond the built-in Cloudflare Pages GitHub integration
Email/DNS provider setup outside of adding a single CNAME record

files.purpose: Cloudflare Pages SPA fallback rule: redirects all unmatched paths to index.html with HTTP 200 so React client-side routing works on hard refresh or direct URL entry

Optional Cloudflare Pages _headers file to set Cache-Control for service worker (sw.js) to `no-store` / `max-age=0` and ensure correct Content-Type, preventing stale SW caching by Cloudflare's CDN edge
Document the two required env vars (VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY) so any developer forking the repo knows what to configure in their own Cloudflare Pages project

implementation_steps.details: Add a single line `/* /index.html 200` to `public/_redirects`. Vite copies everything in `public/` verbatim into `dist/` at build time, so the file will be present at the root of the output directory. Cloudflare Pages reads `_redirects` natively — no wrangler.toml needed. This is required because React's client-side view router (state-based, not URL-based per PRD §6) still needs index.html on direct navigation or refresh to any path the CDN does not recognise as a static asset.

implementation_steps.description: Create public/_headers to pin service worker cache headers
implementation_steps.details:
Add `public/_headers` with the following content:

```
/sw.js
  Cache-Control: no-store, max-age=0
  Content-Type: application/javascript; charset=utf-8

/manifest.webmanifest
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate
  Content-Type: application/manifest+json; charset=utf-8
```

Cloudflare Pages CDN may otherwise serve sw.js with a long cache TTL from its edge, causing users to run a stale service worker for hours after a deploy. `no-store` ensures the browser always fetches the current sw.js on page load, which is the correct behaviour for service worker update semantics.

implementation_steps.description: Connect GitHub repository to Cloudflare Pages
implementation_steps.details: In the Cloudflare dashboard: Pages → Create a project → Connect to Git → select the niyet GitHub repository. Set: Framework preset = Vite, Build command = `npm run build`, Build output directory = `dist`, Root directory = `/` (repo root). Leave Node.js version at the default (currently 18.x LTS; confirm it matches `.nvmrc` or `engines` in package.json if present). Do not enable `--legacy-peer-deps` or other npm flags unless a dependency conflict surfaces in the build log.

implementation_steps.description: Add environment variables in Cloudflare Pages
implementation_steps.details:
After project creation, navigate to the project → Settings → Environment Variables. Add two variables:
- `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` — value: the Supabase project URL (e.g. `https://xxxx.supabase.co`)
- `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` — value: the Supabase anon/public key

Set both for **Production** environment. Also add them for **Preview** environment (same values, or a separate Supabase project for preview if desired — see Q1). These are `VITE_` prefixed so Vite inlines them at build time via `import.meta.env`. They are not secrets in the traditional sense — Supabase RLS protects the data; the anon key is safe to expose (PRD §4.3).

implementation_steps.description: Trigger the first production deploy and verify build success
implementation_steps.details: After configuration, trigger a deploy (either push to main or use the 'Retry deployment' button). Monitor the build log for: (a) successful `npm install`, (b) successful `npm run build` producing a `dist/` directory, (c) correct asset upload count. Confirm the deployment URL (e.g. `https://niyet.pages.dev`) loads the app.

In Cloudflare Pages → the project → Custom Domains → Add a domain → enter `niyet.burakgizlice.com`. Since burakgizlice.com is managed through Cloudflare DNS (assumption: domain is on Cloudflare — see Q2), the dashboard will offer to add the CNAME automatically. Accept. The CNAME record will be:
- Type: CNAME
- Name: niyet
- Target: <project-name>.pages.dev
- Proxy status: Proxied (orange cloud)

If the domain is NOT on Cloudflare DNS, manually add the CNAME at the registrar/DNS provider. SSL certificate is provisioned automatically by Cloudflare; allow up to 24h for propagation though typically it resolves in minutes.

In the Supabase dashboard → Authentication → URL Configuration:
1. Set **Site URL** to `https://niyet.burakgizlice.com`
2. Under **Redirect URLs** add ONLY the following at launch:
   - `https://niyet.burakgizlice.com/**`
   - `http://localhost:5173/**` (for local dev; already added in Block 17 but verify)

Do NOT add `https://*.pages.dev/**` at launch. Preview-deployment auth is explicitly deferred until after production is live and stable (C5 — locked by user). Auth on PR preview URLs will not work initially; this is accepted. Add the wildcard or per-preview entries later if needed.

implementation_steps.description: Verify service worker is served and registers correctly on production domain
implementation_steps.details:
After the custom domain is live:
1. Open Chrome DevTools → Application → Service Workers on https://niyet.burakgizlice.com
2. Confirm sw.js is registered and status is 'activated and is running'
3. In Network tab, confirm sw.js response headers include `Cache-Control: no-store` (from step 19.2)
4. In Application → Manifest, confirm the web app manifest loads without errors and shows the correct app name, theme_color, and icons
5. Test offline: in DevTools → Network → Offline, reload the page — the app shell should load from the service worker cache
6. On a mobile device, test 'Add to Home Screen' (Ana ekrana ekle) — confirm standalone mode opens the app

implementation_steps.description: Verify SPA routing and hard-refresh behaviour
implementation_steps.details:
1. Navigate to the app root at https://niyet.burakgizlice.com — should load.
2. Navigate to a non-root path (e.g. https://niyet.burakgizlice.com/chains) via the in-app navigation — should load the correct view.
3. Hard-refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) on that path — should NOT return a 404; should load index.html and the React app should render the appropriate view (based on the app's own view state, likely falling back to 'main' since routing is state-based per PRD §6, not URL-based — this is acceptable).
4. Confirm the `_redirects` file is present in the deployed `dist/` by checking the Cloudflare Pages deployment file listing.

implementation_steps.description: Verify PR preview deployments work end-to-end
implementation_steps.details:
1. Open a test PR against main.
2. Cloudflare Pages should automatically build and post a preview URL to the PR (via the Cloudflare Pages GitHub App / integration).
3. Visit the preview URL — app should load with the correct env vars (VITE_SUPABASE_URL / VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY from the Preview environment).
4. Confirm the Cloudflare Pages GitHub App is installed on the repository (this is prompted during initial project setup in step 19.3).
5. Test that magic-link auth works on the preview URL (subject to the redirect URL caveat in step 19.7).

Ensure `/.env.example` exists and contains:
```
# Supabase project URL — find in Supabase dashboard → Settings → API
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://your-project-ref.supabase.co

# Supabase anon (public) key — safe to expose; RLS protects the data
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=your-anon-key-here
```

This file is committed to the repo. The actual `.env` file must be in `.gitignore` (standard Vite setup). This prevents accidental credential exposure while documenting what deployers need to configure.

implementation_steps.description: Final deploy checklist and go-live confirmation
implementation_steps.details: Run through the deploy checklist (see acceptance_criteria) top to bottom. Confirm: (a) https://niyet.burakgizlice.com loads the Niyet PWA, (b) Supabase Auth flow completes successfully including redirect back to the production domain, (c) localStorage persistence works across page reloads, (d) service worker is active, (e) app is installable as a PWA on mobile.

data_structures.name: _redirects file content
data_structures.notes: Cloudflare Pages native redirect rule format. The 200 code means 'serve index.html with a 200 status', not a redirect — this is the SPA rewrite semantics. Must be in the root of the `dist/` output directory, which is achieved by placing it in `public/` in the Vite project.

data_structures.name: _headers file content
data_structures.shape: Plain text, Cloudflare Pages headers format: path on own line, then indented header key: value pairs
data_structures.notes: Applies per-path response headers at the CDN edge. Critical for sw.js: service workers must not be cached by intermediary CDNs or the browser will not pick up updates. Also ensures manifest.webmanifest is served with the correct MIME type for PWA installability.

data_structures.name: Cloudflare Pages project configuration
data_structures.shape: { framework_preset: 'vite', build_command: 'npm run build', output_directory: 'dist', root_directory: '/', node_version: '18.x' }
data_structures.notes: Set in Cloudflare dashboard during project creation. There is no committed config file for this — it lives in Cloudflare's own project settings. Document these values in the deploy checklist.

data_structures.name: Environment variables
data_structures.shape: { VITE_SUPABASE_URL: string, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: string } — set for both Production and Preview environments in Cloudflare Pages settings
data_structures.notes: VITE_ prefix causes Vite to inline the values at build time into the JS bundle via `import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL`. They become public in the bundle — this is intentional and safe per Supabase's RLS model.

data_structures.name: Supabase Auth redirect URLs list
data_structures.shape: Array of URL patterns in Supabase dashboard: ['https://niyet.burakgizlice.com/**', 'https://*.pages.dev/**', 'http://localhost:5173/**']
data_structures.notes: Controls which URIs Supabase will accept as auth callback destinations. Incorrect configuration here is the most common auth failure point in Cloudflare/Supabase deployments.

interfaces.name: public/_redirects
interfaces.purpose: Instructs Cloudflare Pages CDN to serve index.html for every request path that does not match a static file, enabling client-side React routing to function on direct navigation and hard refresh

interfaces.name: public/_headers
interfaces.signature:
/sw.js
  Cache-Control: no-store, max-age=0
  Content-Type: application/javascript; charset=utf-8

/manifest.webmanifest
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate
  Content-Type: application/manifest+json; charset=utf-8
interfaces.purpose: Prevents CDN-layer caching of the service worker (which would block updates from propagating to users) and ensures correct MIME types for PWA resources

interfaces.name: Cloudflare Pages GitHub App webhook
interfaces.signature: On push to main → trigger production build; on PR open/update → trigger preview build, post URL as PR status check
interfaces.purpose: Provides auto-deploy and PR preview functionality without any custom CI script

acceptance_criteria: https://niyet.burakgizlice.com loads the Niyet PWA with HTTP 200 and a valid TLS certificate
Build succeeds for `npm run build` in the Cloudflare Pages build environment with no errors
Hard-refreshing any path (e.g. /chains, /auth) returns HTTP 200 and loads the app (not a 404)
The deployed dist/ root contains a `_redirects` file with `/* /index.html 200`
The deployed dist/ root contains a `_headers` file that sets `Cache-Control: no-store` for sw.js
VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY are set in Cloudflare Pages for both Production and Preview environments and are present in `import.meta.env` at runtime
Pushing to main triggers a new production deployment automatically (verified by pushing a trivial change and observing the Cloudflare Pages build log)
Opening a pull request generates a preview deployment with a unique *.pages.dev URL
The service worker (sw.js) is registered and active on https://niyet.burakgizlice.com as confirmed in Chrome DevTools → Application → Service Workers
sw.js is served with `Cache-Control: no-store` (confirmed in DevTools Network tab response headers)
The PWA manifest loads without errors in DevTools → Application → Manifest and shows app name 'Niyet', correct theme_color, and valid icons
The app is installable (Ana ekrana ekle) on an Android or iOS device from the production domain
The app loads from the service worker cache when the network is set to Offline in DevTools
Supabase Auth → URL Configuration shows https://niyet.burakgizlice.com/** and https://*.pages.dev/** in Redirect URLs
Magic-link auth completes successfully and redirects back to https://niyet.burakgizlice.com without a redirect_uri_mismatch error
Google OAuth completes successfully and redirects back to the production domain
DNS CNAME record for niyet.burakgizlice.com resolves to the Cloudflare Pages project (verified with `dig niyet.burakgizlice.com CNAME`)
.env.example exists in the repository root and documents both required env vars
verification: Run `dig niyet.burakgizlice.com CNAME` — should return a CNAME pointing to <project>.pages.dev
Run `curl -I https://niyet.burakgizlice.com` — should return HTTP 200 with `cf-ray:` header confirming Cloudflare edge delivery
Run `curl -I https://niyet.burakgizlice.com/sw.js` — confirm `cache-control: no-store` in response headers
Run `curl https://niyet.burakgizlice.com/nonexistent-path` — should return the index.html content (200, not 404)
Open https://niyet.burakgizlice.com in Chrome Canary → DevTools → Application → Service Workers → confirm status 'activated and is running'
Open DevTools → Application → Manifest → confirm no manifest errors and icons are present
Set DevTools → Network → Offline, reload page — app shell loads from SW cache
On an Android device, open https://niyet.burakgizlice.com in Chrome → tap menu → 'Add to Home Screen' — confirm PWA installs and opens in standalone mode
Trigger magic-link sign-in on the production domain — confirm redirect back to niyet.burakgizlice.com and session is established
Open a test PR → confirm a preview URL is posted as a GitHub status check → visit the preview URL → confirm app loads
Check the Cloudflare Pages deployment file listing for the latest build — confirm `_redirects` and `_headers` files are present in the root
In the Supabase dashboard → Authentication → URL Configuration — visually confirm both redirect URL patterns are present

questionable_decisions.decision: Use the same Supabase project (same VITE_SUPABASE_URL / VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY) for both Production and Preview environments in Cloudflare Pages
questionable_decisions.assumption: PR preview deployments are only used by the developer (burak) and not external contributors, so sharing the production Supabase project for previews is acceptable — there is no risk of preview test data polluting a shared production dataset that multiple users depend on
alternatives: Create a separate Supabase project for preview/staging and use different env vars in the Preview environment — cleaner isolation but doubles Supabase project management overhead
Disable auth in preview deployments entirely — simplest but breaks auth testing in PRs
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Acceptable for a personal/single-author project. Use the same project. If the app gains external contributors, create a staging Supabase project.

questionable_decisions.decision: Assume burakgizlice.com is managed through Cloudflare DNS, enabling the CNAME to be added automatically by the Cloudflare Pages custom domain wizard
questionable_decisions.assumption: The domain is already on Cloudflare (nameservers pointing to Cloudflare). If it is on another DNS provider (e.g. Namecheap, GoDaddy), the CNAME must be added manually at that provider, and the Pages wizard flow is different.
alternatives: Transfer domain to Cloudflare DNS if not already there — strongly recommended for the fastest setup and SSL provisioning
Add a CNAME manually at the current DNS provider and wait for propagation (can take up to 48h)
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Verify Cloudflare nameservers for burakgizlice.com before starting step 19.6. If not on Cloudflare DNS, either transfer the domain or manually add the CNAME. The spec assumes Cloudflare DNS — adjust if necessary.

questionable_decisions.decision: Use Cloudflare Pages native `_redirects` file for SPA fallback rather than a `wrangler.toml` or Pages Functions catch-all route
questionable_decisions.assumption: The `_redirects` file approach is simpler, requires no additional tooling, and is the officially recommended approach for SPAs on Cloudflare Pages. No server-side logic is needed.
alternatives: Use a Pages Function (functions/_middleware.js) to rewrite requests — more powerful but adds complexity and a Functions billing surface
Configure 'Custom 404 page' to serve index.html — does not give a 200 status, which can cause SEO/crawler issues
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Stick with `_redirects`. It is the lightest correct solution.

questionable_decisions.decision: Rely on Cloudflare Pages' built-in GitHub integration for CI/CD rather than a GitHub Actions workflow
questionable_decisions.assumption: The project has no complex build pipeline requirements beyond `npm run build`. The built-in integration is zero-config and sufficient.
alternatives: Use GitHub Actions with `cloudflare/pages-action` — gives more control (e.g., run tests before deploy, post custom PR comments) but adds a workflow file to maintain
Use Cloudflare Workers CI/CD pipeline — overkill for a static SPA
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Built-in integration is correct for this project's scale. If automated test runs before deploy are desired in future, add a GitHub Actions workflow at that point.

questionable_decisions.decision: Add `https://*.pages.dev/**` as a wildcard Supabase Auth redirect URL to cover all PR preview deployments with a single entry
questionable_decisions.assumption: Supabase supports wildcard subdomain patterns in redirect URLs. As of 2025, Supabase's redirect URL matching supports `*` as a wildcard within the path but behaviour with subdomain wildcards (`*.pages.dev`) varies by Supabase version and plan. This must be verified.
alternatives: If wildcard subdomains are not supported: add each preview URL manually (impractical for many PRs) — not recommended
If wildcard subdomains are not supported: use a dedicated Supabase project for previews with `http://localhost` or a fixed preview URL in its allowed list
Disable auth testing on preview deployments and only test auth on production
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Attempt the wildcard entry first. If Supabase rejects it or auth fails on preview URLs, fall back to testing auth only on the production domain for this personal-use project. Document the limitation clearly.
questionable_decisions.resolution: C5 — locked by user: prod domain only at launch; preview/wildcard auth deferred until after prod is live.

open_questions: Is burakgizlice.com currently using Cloudflare nameservers? This determines whether the CNAME for niyet.burakgizlice.com can be auto-created via the Pages dashboard or must be added manually at a third-party DNS provider.
Does the current Supabase plan support wildcard subdomain patterns (*.pages.dev) in Auth redirect URLs? This determines whether PR preview deployments can use magic-link/OAuth auth.

Is there a `.nvmrc` or `engines` field in package.json specifying a Node.js version? Cloudflare Pages defaults to Node 18 but the project may require a specific version — check and set NODE_VERSION env var if needed.

Should preview deployments use the same Supabase project as production, or a separate staging project? The spec defaults to shared, which is fine for a solo project but should be reconsidered if external contributors send PRs.

Does the app need any server-side headers beyond what `_headers` provides? For example, Content-Security-Policy headers — not required for v1 but worth considering before going public after the YouTube video.
Are there any large binary assets (icons, fonts) in the repo that would significantly slow the Cloudflare Pages build? The build log from step 19.5 will surface this.
